name: Latest health certificate (document reference, date, attachment)
pageTitle: Latest Health Certificate - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Documents
heading: Latest Health Certificate
bodyCopy: Make sure the health certificate covers all commodities in this notification, or the consignment may be rejected.
This is the document that will be shown on the Common Health Entry Document (CHED).
If you do not have a validated e-health certificate, you must still provide the original health certificate at the Border Control Post
If the attachments field is blank, your document is missing. You must upload it manually.
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (an empty govuk-grid-column-two-thirds govuk-!-padding-0 precedes the row)

structure.govukComponent: additional-documents__caption rendered as govuk-heading-m (bespoke table caption)

structure.text: Make sure the health certificate covers all commodities in this notification, or the consignment may be rejected.
This is the document that will be shown on the Common Health Entry Document (CHED).
If you do not have a validated e-health certificate, you must still provide the original health certificate at the Border Control Post
If the attachments field is blank, your document is missing. You must upload it manually.
Document type / Document reference / Date of issue (Day Month Year) / Attachments

structure.govukComponent: Body small (govuk-body govuk-body-s) — the fixed Document type value for this single row

structure.text: Document issue date (Day / Month / Year) with a Choose date picker
structure.govukComponent: Date input (govuk-date-input) inside govuk-fieldset with visually-hidden legend, plus custom defra-datepicker

structure.text: View <filename> (attachment link — rendered in the Attachments column only after a successful upload; NOT seen in the corpus trace)
structure.govukComponent: Link (govuk-link) — INFERRED from page object + valid-upload test, not trace-observed

govukComponents.usage: Two intro paragraphs, the static 'Veterinary health certificate' cell (govuk-body-s), and the Cancel-and-return link paragraph
Legal warning about providing the original health certificate at the BCP
Blank-attachment warning ('you must upload it manually')
Groups the Day/Month/Year date inputs; legend 'Document issue date' is visually hidden
Date of issue — three text inputs Day (width-2), Month (width-2), Year (width-4)
Document reference (full-ish width) plus the three date-part inputs
Wrappers for the reference input, the date fieldset and each date part
'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' submit buttons
'Cancel and return to hub' link to the notification overview

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: additional-documents-table / additional-documents__grid-row / additional-documents__grid-column-15 / __grid-column-35 / __grid-column-doc-date / additional-documents__headers / additional-documents__table-header / additional-documents__caption
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke CSS-grid 'table' laying out the document row into four columns (Document type | Document reference | Date of issue | Attachments) with a header row. On this page it holds a SINGLE fixed row for the veterinary health certificate.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System component — it is a hand-rolled grid masquerading as a table, with custom column-width classes (15/35/doc-date). Reused across the supporting/additional-documents pages. The header/hint markup (Day Month Year spans) is bespoke rather than govuk date-input hints.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: For a single fixed-type document the row could be a plain set of govuk form fields (govuk-input + govuk-date-input) under headings, or the Summary list / Table component if a genuine multi-row list is needed. The new IUU app should model 'the health certificate' as one document object with reference + issue date + attachment, not as a generic editable table.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: defra-datepicker / date-picker__container / date-picker__dialog / date-picker__reveal__icon / date-picker__date-table / date-input--picker / date-picker-day|month|year / data-module="accessible-datepicker"
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A custom JavaScript accessible date picker (calendar dialog with previous/next month, choose-date button, close/Cancel) layered on top of the govuk-date-input Day/Month/Year fields.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Third-party / bespoke widget, not part of govuk-frontend. Adds a modal calendar dialog, an SVG calendar icon (uses #calendar sprite), aria-live regions and sr-only utility classes. Progressive-enhancement JS dependency; behaviour and a11y are IPAFFS-specific.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK guidance for a known date is the plain Date input component (three text fields) with no calendar widget. The new app can drop the picker and keep the standard govuk-date-input.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A submit button styled to look like a link, used as the 'Add attachment' action inside the Attachments column. Submits the form with name=add-attachment-latest-health-cert value=latest-vet-health to route to the file-upload sub-page.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom 'link-button' styling is not a Design System class; a link-styled submit button mixes button and link affordances. 'no-wrap' is a bespoke utility to keep the label on one line.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK Button (optionally govuk-button--secondary) for an action that submits, or a proper button-as-link only via the documented pattern. In the new app, adding the attachment is a form submit and should use a govuk-button.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Page-specific styling hooks appended to the govuk-input / date-input / attachment elements.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke per-page class overrides sitting alongside govuk-* classes — styling divergence from the toolbox. Low risk individually but signals custom CSS.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Rely on govuk-input / govuk-date-input defaults and govuk spacing utilities instead of page-specific override classes.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Screen-reader-only utility classes used by the custom datepicker (Choose date label, live-region announcements).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-govuk visually-hidden utility (GOV.UK uses govuk-visually-hidden); introduced by the bespoke datepicker rather than the app itself.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-visually-hidden — becomes moot if the custom datepicker is removed.

fields.name: latest-vet-health-cert-reference
fields.label: Document reference
fields.conditionalOn: Whole page is conditional on risk category — rendered only when riskCategory !== 'Low risk'; on the low-risk path the page is skipped and the journey routes straight to Accompanying Documents (ched-p-workflows.ts:339-354, 720-732; page-object driver getByRole textbox 'Document reference')
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 66/68 (aria-label 'Document reference'; no HTML5 required attribute). Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:7-9 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Document reference' })); DEFAULT_CONFIG value 'REF-123' at ched-p-workflows.ts:134; filled by every workflow variant (ched-p-workflows.ts:341, 722, 931)

fields.name: Document type (static)
fields.label: Document type
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 68 — rendered as fixed body text 'Veterinary health certificate', NOT an editable control on this page (unlike the generic supporting-documents page which uses a Document type <select>). Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts exposes NO Document type control (only reference, day/month/year, add-attachment), consistent with a fixed type — whereas AccompanyingDocumentsPage exposes a dropdownDocumentType (ched-p-workflows.ts:357)

fields.name: latest-vet-health-cert-issue-date-day
fields.label: Day
fields.conditionalOn: part of the 'Document issue date' fieldset (legend visually hidden); maxlength=2, inputmode=numeric
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 67 (Fill '04'). Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:11-13 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Day' })), filled via fillHealthCertificateDate(); DEFAULT_CONFIG healthCertificateDate.day='04' at ched-p-workflows.ts:136

fields.name: latest-vet-health-cert-issue-date-month
fields.label: Month
fields.conditionalOn: part of the 'Document issue date' fieldset; maxlength=2, inputmode=numeric
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 68 (Fill '03') — the evidence-pointer action. Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:15-17 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Month' })); DEFAULT_CONFIG healthCertificateDate.month='03' at ched-p-workflows.ts:137

fields.name: latest-vet-health-cert-issue-date-year
fields.label: Year
fields.conditionalOn: part of the 'Document issue date' fieldset; maxlength=4, inputmode=numeric
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 69 (Fill '2023'). Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:19-21 (getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Year' })); DEFAULT_CONFIG healthCertificateDate.year='2023' at ched-p-workflows.ts:138

fields.name: add-attachment-latest-health-cert
fields.label: Add attachment
fields.conditionalOn: clicking submits the form (value=latest-vet-health) and routes to a separate file-upload sub-page (Document Upload Page — Set input files on input[type=file] then Continue/Next) — the attachment itself is uploaded on that sub-page, not inline here. Accepted file types (from valid-upload test): jpg, png, doc, docx, xls, pdf. Max size 10MB; empty files rejected (see validationMessages). After a successful upload the Attachments column renders a 'View <filename>' link (see the attachment-link element)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 70 (Click 'Add attachment') then action 71 (Set input files on input[type=file]) + action 72 (Continue). Corroborated: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:27-29 (btnAddAttachment getByRole button 'Add attachment'); DocumentUploadPage.ts:14-24 (btnNextOrContinue, inputFile); ched-p-workflows.ts:346-350, 724-728 upload example.png

fields.name: latest-vet-health-cert-attachment-link
fields.label: View <filename>
fields.conditionalOn: Rendered in the Attachments column ONLY after a file has been successfully uploaded on the Document Upload sub-page and the flow returns to this page. Not present on first render (the corpus trace uploaded but did not exercise the returned link). Label is 'View ' + the uploaded filename (e.g. 'View example.png')
fields.evidence: LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:23-25 (locatorAttachmentLink(filename) = getByRole('link', { name: `View ${filename}` })); asserted visible after upload in document-upload-valid-health-certificate.spec.ts:21 and :36 — a behaviour no corpus trace rendered

validationMessages.trigger: Whether Document reference and the issue date are mandatory on THIS page could not be confirmed: the corpus trace has 0 errors, there are no HTML5 required attributes, and no QA test drives an empty reference/date to assert an error. Every workflow fills both, which suggests they are expected, but the required rule and its error copy are unconfirmed.
validationMessages.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 68 — no govuk-error-summary present; controls have required=false. No assertion in tests/notification/ched-p/ or the accessibility spec drives an empty-field error on this page

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS
validationMessages.trigger: Uploading an attachment whose type is not permitted (e.g. .zip, .txt). Error surfaces in the error summary (role=alert) on the Document Upload sub-page reached via 'Add attachment', NOT inline on this page.
validationMessages.evidence: document-upload-invalid-health-certificate.spec.ts:7-8,26,41 (asserts locatorErrorSummary toContainText for example.zip / example.txt). Not observed in the corpus trace

validationMessages.trigger: Uploading an empty file (empty-file.pdf). Error surfaces in the error summary on the Document Upload sub-page.
validationMessages.evidence: document-upload-invalid-health-certificate.spec.ts:9,26,41. Not observed in the corpus trace

validationMessages.message: The selected file must be smaller than 10MB
validationMessages.trigger: Uploading a file larger than 10MB (large-file.pdf). Error surfaces in the error summary on the Document Upload sub-page.
validationMessages.evidence: document-upload-invalid-health-certificate.spec.ts:10,26,41. Not observed in the corpus trace

openQuestions: The real URL slug is 'latest-certificate' (form action='latest-certificate'; returnUrls end .../DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/latest-certificate), NOT 'latest-health-certificate' as the page slug/url-pattern in the task suggested. The page is titled/headed 'Latest Health Certificate' but the route is /latest-certificate.

This page is the FIXED health-certificate variant of the shared additional-documents table: Document type is hard-coded to 'Veterinary health certificate' (static text, no select). The generic supporting-documents page immediately after (action 74) DOES render a 'Document type' <select> — so the two share markup (additional-documents-table) but differ in whether Document type is editable.

No field is marked required in HTML; validation is server-side and no error state was captured in this passing trace. Whether Document reference / issue date / attachment are mandatory, and the exact error copy, are GAPS needing an error-state trace or the page object / template to confirm.

The task placed this at journey position 16 and noted the trace mislabels it 'Supporting documents (1st)'. Confirmed: it sits AFTER the catch-certificate loop (actions 42-65) and BEFORE the generic supporting-documents page (action 74). It is its own dedicated 'Latest Health Certificate' page.

The Date of issue uses a bespoke defra-datepicker (calendar dialog) over the govuk-date-input; the new app should confirm whether a calendar widget is required or the plain GOV.UK Date input suffices.

Only one row is present (single health certificate). Whether multiple attachments per health certificate are supported was not exercised — after 'Add attachment' the flow returns to a row showing the attachment; not captured here.

PAGE-LEVEL CONDITIONALITY (inferred, high-value): the whole Latest Health Certificate page is gated on risk category. It is rendered only when riskCategory !== 'Low risk' (default journeys use 'Medium risk'); on the low-risk path the page is skipped entirely and the journey routes straight to Accompanying Documents. Confirmed by the QA workflow guards ched-p-workflows.ts:339-354, 720-732 and the comment at :930 ('required after catch certificates on medium risk'). The new IUU app must decide whether this page appears unconditionally or is risk-gated. Trace corpus only exercised the medium/high-risk (page-present) path.

The attachment upload is a two-step flow: 'Add attachment' submits to a separate Document Upload sub-page (govuk file input + Continue/Next). Accepted types per the valid-upload test are jpg, png, doc, docx, xls, pdf; the error copy 'The selected file must be a DOC, JPEG, PDF, PNG or XLS' names DOC/JPEG/PDF/PNG/XLS (note DOCX and XLS/XLSX phrasing differs between the accepted-list and the error string — worth confirming exact allowed extensions with a human). Max size is 10MB and empty files are rejected. These validations live on the Document Upload sub-page, not on this page.

Required rule for Document reference and issue date is a GAP: no error-state trace and no QA test drives an empty field to assert an error on this page. Every workflow fills both (values REF-123 and 04/03/2023 from DEFAULT_CONFIG), which suggests they are expected/mandatory, but the actual server-side rule and its error copy are unconfirmed and need a human or an error-state trace.

After a successful upload the Attachments column renders a 'View <filename>' link (LatestHealthCertificatePage.ts:23-25, asserted in document-upload-valid-health-certificate.spec.ts). No corpus trace captured this returned state — it is inferred from the page object and test only.
